'''Max Wood''' (born 28 June 2004) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Hull FC]], on loan from the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>[https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/max-wood-61064.html itsrugby profile]</ref>

He was contracted to the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]]. Wood has spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Bradford Bulls]] and the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>[https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/24213180.max-wood-joins-bradford-bulls-loan-warrington-wolves/ Max Wood joins Bradford Bulls on loan from Warrington Wolves]</ref>

==Background==
Wood was born in [[Bury, Greater Manchester]], England.<ref>[https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/released-wigan-warriors-youngster-secures-long-term-future-with-new-super-league-contract-4791127 Released Wigan Warriors youngster secures long-term future with new Super League contract]</ref>

He played for [[Rochdale Mayfield]] as a junior.<ref>[https://www.pressreader.com/uk/rochdale-observer/20240608/282166476330991 Ex-mayfield duo set for Final]</ref> He played for [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] in the [[Rugby League War of the Roses|Origin series]].

==Career==
Wood was contracted to the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and spent part of 2023 on loan at the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].<ref>[https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2023/06/23/three-warriors-join-hurricanes-on-loan/ Three Warriors join Hurricanes on loan]</ref>

In 2024 Wood made his début for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[Super League]] against [[Hull FC]].<ref>[https://warringtonwolves.com/news/2024/february/Heritage-number-confirmed-for-Max-Wood/ Heritage number confirmed for Max Wood]</ref>
